collections.title: A courtyard wedding
collections.promptTemplate:
[# References {{REFERENCE_TAGS}}]
Create a 10-second multi-shot cinematic montage that progresses chronologically through this one wedding evening, from the ceremony's last warm daylight into the sparkler-lit night.
[0-3s] An establishing shot brings the earliest selected photo to life: linen and leaves stir in the courtyard air as the newlyweds settle into the moment; use the earliest real photo as the visual anchor.
[3-7s] Cut through the middle selected photos as connected candid beats—the same newlyweds crossing the courtyard, sharing a toast, then finding each other on the dance floor. Use gentle handheld camera movement and motivated match cuts through gestures, warm window light, and swaying fabric; let each supplied photo become a recognizable anchor moment in chronological order.
[7-10s] Cut to the latest selected photo in the deep-blue evening, ending close on the newlyweds together as sparklers glow behind them and the camera eases to stillness.
The same two newlywed adults are the recurring visual anchors in every shot. Their facial identity, age, hair, body proportions, wedding clothes, and jewelry must persist across every cut; do not redesign, merge, duplicate, age, or replace either person. Keep the stone courtyard, adult guests, flowers, and changing light coherent. Natural ambient sound only, no dialogue, no captions. Use the supplied real photos as chronological identity, wardrobe, setting, and event references; preserve their composition at anchor moments, but create connective cinematic motion between them rather than using them as literal initial frames.

photos.alt: Two newlyweds walking from a stone courtyard ceremony in soft afternoon light
The newlyweds sharing a quiet portrait beneath olive branches
The newlyweds laughing during a courtyard toast with adult friends
The newlyweds beginning their first dance under warm string lights
The newlyweds dancing close as blue hour settles over the courtyard
The newlyweds together at a sparkler send-off beneath the night sky
The newlyweds walk away down a lantern-lit gravel lane after the sparkler send-off.
The newlyweds rest their foreheads together beneath an olive tree, the inn glowing far behind.

collections.title: Forty-two, after dark
collections.promptTemplate:
[# References {{REFERENCE_TAGS}}]
Create a 10-second multi-shot cinematic montage that progresses chronologically through this one adults-only loft birthday, from late-afternoon preparations into a candlelit night.
[0-3s] An establishing shot brings the earliest selected photo to life: window light moves across the loft, paper garlands lift slightly, and the birthday host and his partner finish setting the long table; use the earliest real photo as the visual anchor.
[3-7s] Cut through the middle selected photos as intimate, energetic beats—the same pair welcoming adult friends, leaning into a toast, and laughing as the cake candles flare. Connect each supplied anchor moment in chronological order with a slow push-in, a clean cut on raised glasses, and warm practical light gathering around the room.
[7-10s] Cut to the latest selected photo after dinner, ending on the same pair dancing by the rain-dark windows while their adult friends soften into the background and city reflections shimmer.
The birthday host and his partner are the recurring visual anchors in every shot. Preserve their exact facial identity, age, hair, body proportions, and clothing across every cut; do not redesign, merge, duplicate, age, or replace either person. Keep all guests unmistakably adult and keep the loft, table setting, cake, and coral-amber-teal palette coherent as daylight falls. Natural ambient sound only, no dialogue, no captions. Use the supplied real photos as chronological identity and event references; return visibly to their compositions as anchor moments while inventing only restrained connective cinematic motion, never treating the photos as literal initial frames.

photos.alt: A man and his partner setting a colorful birthday table in a sunlit loft
The couple welcoming adult friends into the loft birthday party
The birthday host and his partner raising glasses at the dinner table
The birthday host laughing over a candlelit citrus cake
The couple sharing a quiet moment after the birthday toast
The couple dancing beside rain-dark loft windows after dinner

collections.title: The long way north
collections.promptTemplate:
[# References {{REFERENCE_TAGS}}]
Create a 10-second multi-shot cinematic road-film montage that progresses chronologically through this one coastal day, from a pale morning departure to blue hour above the ocean.
[0-3s] An establishing shot brings the earliest selected photo to life: sea mist drifts past the parked wagon as the same two friends unfold a paper map and morning wind catches their jackets; use the earliest real photo as the visual anchor.
[3-7s] Cut through the middle selected photos as connected travel beats—the wagon moving along the cliff road, the same friends crossing a windswept overlook, then sharing coffee at a weathered roadside stop. Let every supplied photo appear as a recognizable anchor in chronological order, joined by restrained tracking motion, a match cut on the map, and passing bands of silver-blue and late-golden light.
[7-10s] Cut to the latest selected photo at blue hour, ending wide on the same two friends beside the wagon above the water as the last horizon light fades and the camera gently pulls back.
The same two adult friends are the recurring visual anchors in every shot. Preserve their exact facial identity, age, hair, body proportions, layered clothing, and accessories across every cut; do not redesign, merge, duplicate, age, or replace either person. Keep the seafoam wagon, rugged coastline, weather, and sea-salt-blue, sage, and rust palette coherent. Natural ambient sound only, no dialogue, no captions. Use the supplied real photos as chronological identity, wardrobe, vehicle, and location references; honor their compositions at anchor moments and create plausible connective cinematic motion between them rather than using them as literal initial frames.

photos.alt: Two friends unfolding a map beside a seafoam wagon in the morning mist
The friends leaning from the parked wagon at a bright coastal turnout
The friends walking together across a windswept cliff overlook
The friends sharing coffee outside a weathered coastal roadside stop
The friends beside the wagon as golden light reaches the cliffs
The friends watching the ocean beside their wagon at blue hour
